title: Smoky Roasted Ethiopian Fish Pilaf
description: A flavorful and aromatic Ethiopian-inspired pilaf featuring smoked fish, spaghetti, and a blend of spices, perfect for a brunch or light meal.

ingredients.name: 2 smoked fish fillets (such as mackerel or salmon)

instructions: Cook the spaghetti in a large pot of salted water until al dente, then set aside.
In a large skillet, heat the oil over medium heat. Add the onion and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the garlic, cumin, smoked paprika, coriander, and red pepper flakes (if using) to the skillet. Cook for 1 minute.
Add the cooked spaghetti to the skillet and toss with the spice mixture until well combined.
Season the spaghetti mixture with salt and pepper to taste.
Transfer the spaghetti mixture to a baking dish and top with the smoked fish fillets.
Bake in the preheated oven for 10-12 minutes, or until the fish is cooked through and the spaghetti is heated through.
Remove the dish from the oven and sprinkle with parsley, cilantro, and lemon juice.
Serve hot, garnished with additional parsley and lemon wedges if desired.
